query: North Korea nuclear test 2026 - any signs of imminent 7th nuclear test, activity at Punggye-ri test site, recent statements or preparations mid-2026
answer:
Research complete. Summary of findings on North Korea's potential 7th nuclear test as of mid-2026:

**Bottom line:** No 7th test has occurred (last test: Sept 2017). Punggye-ri's Tunnel No. 3 is technically ready and has been for years, but there are **no open-source signs of imminent test preparations**. Any test is purely a political decision by Kim Jong Un.

**Key findings:**
- **Nov 5, 2025** — South Korea's Defense Intelligence Agency assessed North Korea "appears ready to promptly carry out" a 7th test at Tunnel No. 3 "in a short time" if Kim decides. This is a *capability* read, not evidence of imminence.
- **Satellite analysis** (CSIS/Beyond Parallel, 38 North) consistently shows only routine caretaker maintenance — "no indications of test preparations."
- **Feb 2026** — 9th Party Congress reaffirmed "irreversible" nuclear-state status and a new five-year nuclear expansion plan.
- **June 3–4, 2026** — Kim inspected a new uranium enrichment plant, vowing "exponential" arsenal growth and claiming material production "more than doubled" in five years — but **no mention of a test**. VERTIC estimates ~2.4–2.5 tonnes cumulative HEU by end-2026.
- **Analytical caveat** — 38 North argues a 7th test may not even be technically necessary; the Hwasan-31 tactical warhead has never been tested.
- **Pattern** — Multiple past "imminent test" predictions (e.g., 2022) never materialized; readiness ≠ imminence.

North Korea's 2026 nuclear emphasis has been on **fissile-material production and arsenal expansion**, not detonation testing. The full report, structured data points, and 14 sources have been recorded via the StructuredOutput tool.

key_facts: North Korea's last (6th) nuclear test was September 3, 2017; no 7th test has occurred as of mid-2026 (~9 years gap).
Punggye-ri Tunnel No. 3 (South Portal) is technically ready; US and South Korea have assessed all test preparations complete since ~2020-2022.

South Korea's Defense Intelligence Agency (Nov 5, 2025) said North Korea 'appears ready to promptly carry out' a 7th test at Tunnel No. 3 in a short time IF Kim decides — a capability, not a decision.

CSIS/Beyond Parallel and 38 North satellite imagery analyses consistently report only routine caretaker maintenance and 'no indications of test preparations'; most recent detailed imagery report is January 2024.
At the February 2026 9th Party Congress, North Korea reaffirmed 'irreversible' nuclear-state status and adopted a new five-year nuclear force-expansion plan.
On June 3-4, 2026, Kim Jong Un inspected a newly commissioned uranium enrichment/nuclear material plant, vowing to bolster nuclear forces 'at an exponential rate' — no mention of a nuclear test.

Kim claimed weapons-grade nuclear material production capacity 'more than doubled' over five years; VERTIC estimates total ~66 cascades / ~375 kg HEU-equivalent per year and a plausible HEU stockpile of ~2.4-2.5 tonnes by end-2026.
April 2026: IAEA DG Rafael Grossi confirmed 'a rapid increase' in activity at North Korean nuclear facilities.
38 North analysis (Feb/Jun 2026) argues a 7th test may not be technically necessary; the Hwasan-31 tactical warhead (unveiled March 2023) has never been tested.
The decision to test rests solely with Kim Jong Un; multiple past intelligence-based 'imminent test' predictions (e.g. NIS Oct 16-Nov 7, 2022) never materialized.

2026 North Korean nuclear emphasis is on fissile-material production and arsenal expansion, not detonation testing; Kim has rebuffed US/ROK dialogue since 2019 and demands nuclear-state recognition before talks.
